questions.subject: English & Logical Reasoning
questions.text: There are six persons A . B , C , D , E and F . C is the sister of F . B is the brother of E 's husband. D is the father of A and grandfather of F. There are two fathers, three brothers and a mother in the group. Who is the mother?
questions.questionText: There are six persons A . B , C , D , E and F . C is the sister of F . B is the brother of E 's husband. D is the father of A and grandfather of F. There are two fathers, three brothers and a mother in the group. Who is the mother?
questions.question: There are six persons A . B , C , D , E and F . C is the sister of F . B is the brother of E 's husband. D is the father of A and grandfather of F. There are two fathers, three brothers and a mother in the group. Who is the mother?
questions.prompt: There are six persons A . B , C , D , E and F . C is the sister of F . B is the brother of E 's husband. D is the father of A and grandfather of F. There are two fathers, three brothers and a mother in the group. Who is the mother?
questions.html: There are six persons A.B,C,D,E and F. C is the sister of F. B is the brother of E 's husband. D is the father of A and grandfather of F. There are two fathers, three brothers and a mother in the group. Who is the mother?
questions.questionHtml: There are six persons A.B,C,D,E and F. C is the sister of F. B is the brother of E 's husband. D is the father of A and grandfather of F. There are two fathers, three brothers and a mother in the group. Who is the mother?
questions.solutionText: Solution C is the sister of F. B is the brother of E's husband ⇒ E is married; E's husband has a brother B ⇒ B and E's husband are brothers. D is the father of A and grandfather of F ⇒ D is A's father; A is F's parent. Since C is the sister of F, and A is F's parent ⇒ A is the parent of both F and C. D is grandfather of F ⇒ D is parent of A. Group has two fathers: D (father of A, grandfather of F) and A (father/mother of F and C). Since there are two fathers ⇒ both D and A are male ⇒ A is father of F and C. B is brother of E's husband. E's husband must be A (father of F and C). ⇒ B is brother of A ⇒ B, A, and D are related (D is father of A and B). Three brothers: A, B, and one more. The group is A, B, C, D, E, F. C and F are siblings (C is sister of F) ⇒ C is female. E is married to A ⇒ E is female ⇒ E is the mother of F and C. Answer: (4) E
questions.solution: Solution C is the sister of F. B is the brother of E's husband ⇒ E is married; E's husband has a brother B ⇒ B and E's husband are brothers. D is the father of A and grandfather of F ⇒ D is A's father; A is F's parent. Since C is the sister of F, and A is F's parent ⇒ A is the parent of both F and C. D is grandfather of F ⇒ D is parent of A. Group has two fathers: D (father of A, grandfather of F) and A (father/mother of F and C). Since there are two fathers ⇒ both D and A are male ⇒ A is father of F and C. B is brother of E's husband. E's husband must be A (father of F and C). ⇒ B is brother of A ⇒ B, A, and D are related (D is father of A and B). Three brothers: A, B, and one more. The group is A, B, C, D, E, F. C and F are siblings (C is sister of F) ⇒ C is female. E is married to A ⇒ E is female ⇒ E is the mother of F and C. Answer: (4) E
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
C is the sister of F.
B is the brother of E's husband ⇒ E is married; E's husband has a brother B ⇒ B and E's husband are brothers.
D is the father of A and grandfather of F ⇒ D is A's father; A is F's parent.

Since C is the sister of F, and A is F's parent ⇒ A is the parent of both F and C.
D is grandfather of F ⇒ D is parent of A.

Group has two fathers: D (father of A, grandfather of F) and A (father/mother of F and C).
Since there are two fathers ⇒ both D and A are male ⇒ A is father of F and C.

B is brother of E's husband. E's husband must be A (father of F and C).
⇒ B is brother of A ⇒ B, A, and D are related (D is father of A and B).

Three brothers: A, B, and one more. The group is A, B, C, D, E, F.
C and F are siblings (C is sister of F) ⇒ C is female.
E is married to A ⇒ E is female ⇒ E is the mother of F and C.

Answer: (4) E

questions.text: If A + B means A is the mother of B ; A − B means A is the brother B ; A % B means A is the father of B and A × B means A is the sister of B , which of the following shows that P is the maternal uncle of Q ?
questions.questionText: If A + B means A is the mother of B ; A − B means A is the brother B ; A % B means A is the father of B and A × B means A is the sister of B , which of the following shows that P is the maternal uncle of Q ?
questions.question: If A + B means A is the mother of B ; A − B means A is the brother B ; A % B means A is the father of B and A × B means A is the sister of B , which of the following shows that P is the maternal uncle of Q ?
questions.prompt: If A + B means A is the mother of B ; A − B means A is the brother B ; A % B means A is the father of B and A × B means A is the sister of B , which of the following shows that P is the maternal uncle of Q ?
questions.html: If A+B means A is the mother of B;A−B means A is the brother B;A % B means A is the father of B and A×B means A is the sister of B, which of the following shows that P is the maternal uncle of Q ?
questions.questionHtml: If A+B means A is the mother of B;A−B means A is the brother B;A % B means A is the father of B and A×B means A is the sister of B, which of the following shows that P is the maternal uncle of Q ?
questions.solutionText: Solution Decoding the symbols: A + B ⇒ A is mother of B A − B ⇒ A is brother of B A % B ⇒ A is father of B A × B ⇒ A is sister of B For P to be maternal uncle of Q, P must be the brother of Q's mother. Checking option (3): P − M + N × Q N × Q ⇒ N is sister of Q M + N ⇒ M is mother of N, hence mother of Q's sister ⇒ M is mother of Q P − M ⇒ P is brother of M So P is brother of M, and M is mother of Q ⇒ P is maternal uncle of Q Answer: (3) P − M + N × Q
questions.solution: Solution Decoding the symbols: A + B ⇒ A is mother of B A − B ⇒ A is brother of B A % B ⇒ A is father of B A × B ⇒ A is sister of B For P to be maternal uncle of Q, P must be the brother of Q's mother. Checking option (3): P − M + N × Q N × Q ⇒ N is sister of Q M + N ⇒ M is mother of N, hence mother of Q's sister ⇒ M is mother of Q P − M ⇒ P is brother of M So P is brother of M, and M is mother of Q ⇒ P is maternal uncle of Q Answer: (3) P − M + N × Q
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Decoding the symbols:
A+B ⇒ A is mother of B
A−B ⇒ A is brother of B
A % B ⇒ A is father of B
A×B ⇒ A is sister of B

For P to be maternal uncle of Q, P must be the brother of Q's mother.

Checking option (3): P−M+N×Q
N×Q ⇒ N is sister of Q
M+N ⇒ M is mother of N, hence mother of Q's sister ⇒ M is mother of Q
P−M ⇒ P is brother of M

So P is brother of M, and M is mother of Q ⇒ P is maternal uncle of Q

Answer: (3) P−M+N×Q

questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
X={4n−3n−1:n∈N}
X={(1+3)n−3n−1:n∈N}
X={1+3n+32n(n−1)2!+33n(n−1)(2n−1)6....3n−1}
X={32(n−1)n(12!+3(2n−1)6+.....)}
X={9(n−1)n(12!+......)}
Y={9(n−1):n∈N}
∴X⊆Y
∴X∩Y=X
(or)
Put n=1,2,3,4,5,6,..... in X and Y
X={0,9,54,....}
Y={0,9,18,27,36,45,54,63......}
X⊆Y
∴X∩Y=X

questions.text: Consider the function f ( x ) defined as: f ( x ) = { k x 2 for x ≤ 2 8 x − 8 for x > 2 If f ( x ) is differentiable at x = 2 , what is the value of the constant k
questions.questionText: Consider the function f ( x ) defined as: f ( x ) = { k x 2 for x ≤ 2 8 x − 8 for x > 2 If f ( x ) is differentiable at x = 2 , what is the value of the constant k
questions.question: Consider the function f ( x ) defined as: f ( x ) = { k x 2 for x ≤ 2 8 x − 8 for x > 2 If f ( x ) is differentiable at x = 2 , what is the value of the constant k
questions.prompt: Consider the function f ( x ) defined as: f ( x ) = { k x 2 for x ≤ 2 8 x − 8 for x > 2 If f ( x ) is differentiable at x = 2 , what is the value of the constant k
questions.html:
Consider the function f(x) defined as:

f(x)={kx2for x≤28x−8for x>2

If f(x) is differentiable at x=2, what is the value of the constant k
questions.questionHtml:
Consider the function f(x) defined as:

f(x)={kx2for x≤28x−8for x>2

If f(x) is differentiable at x=2, what is the value of the constant k
questions.solutionText: Solution For f ( x ) to be differentiable at x = 2 , it must be continuous and have equal derivatives from both sides. Continuity at x = 2 : k x 2 | x = 2 = 8 x − 8 | x = 2 4 k = 8 ( 2 ) − 8 = 8 k = 2 Verify equal derivatives: d d x ( k x 2 ) = 2 k x ⇒ at x = 2 : 2 ( 2 ) ( 2 ) = 8 d d x ( 8 x − 8 ) = 8 Both derivatives equal 8 , so differentiability is confirmed. Answer: (B) 2
questions.solution: Solution For f ( x ) to be differentiable at x = 2 , it must be continuous and have equal derivatives from both sides. Continuity at x = 2 : k x 2 | x = 2 = 8 x − 8 | x = 2 4 k = 8 ( 2 ) − 8 = 8 k = 2 Verify equal derivatives: d d x ( k x 2 ) = 2 k x ⇒ at x = 2 : 2 ( 2 ) ( 2 ) = 8 d d x ( 8 x − 8 ) = 8 Both derivatives equal 8 , so differentiability is confirmed. Answer: (B) 2
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
For f(x) to be differentiable at x=2, it must be continuous and have equal derivatives from both sides.

Continuity at x=2:
kx2|x=2=8x−8|x=2
4k=8(2)−8=8
k=2

Verify equal derivatives:
ddx(kx2)=2kx⇒ at x=2: 2(2)(2)=8
ddx(8x−8)=8

Both derivatives equal 8, so differentiability is confirmed.

Answer: (B) 2

questions.text: The maximum slope of the curve y = - x 3 + 3 x 2 - 4 x + 9 is

questions.solutionText: Solution Slope of the curve: d y d x = − 3 x 2 + 6 x − 4 For maximum slope, differentiate again and set to zero: d 2 y d x 2 = − 6 x + 6 = 0 ⇒ x = 1 d 3 y d x 3 = − 6 < 0 ⇒ confirms maximum at x = 1 Maximum slope = − 3 ( 1 ) 2 + 6 ( 1 ) − 4 = − 3 + 6 − 4 = − 1 Answer: (2) − 1
questions.solution: Solution Slope of the curve: d y d x = − 3 x 2 + 6 x − 4 For maximum slope, differentiate again and set to zero: d 2 y d x 2 = − 6 x + 6 = 0 ⇒ x = 1 d 3 y d x 3 = − 6 < 0 ⇒ confirms maximum at x = 1 Maximum slope = − 3 ( 1 ) 2 + 6 ( 1 ) − 4 = − 3 + 6 − 4 = − 1 Answer: (2) − 1
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Slope of the curve: dydx=−3x2+6x−4

For maximum slope, differentiate again and set to zero:
d2ydx2=−6x+6=0⇒x=1

d3ydx3=−6<0 ⇒ confirms maximum at x=1

Maximum slope =−3(1)2+6(1)−4=−3+6−4=−1

Answer: (2) −1

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: The solution of x d y d x = y + x e y x with y 1 = 0 is
questions.questionText: The solution of x d y d x = y + x e y x with y 1 = 0 is
questions.question: The solution of x d y d x = y + x e y x with y 1 = 0 is
questions.prompt: The solution of x d y d x = y + x e y x with y 1 = 0 is
questions.html: The solution of xdydx=y+xeyx with y1=0 is
questions.questionHtml: The solution of xdydx=y+xeyx with y1=0 is
questions.solutionText: Solution Given differential equation is x d y d x = y + x e y x d y d x = y x + e y x It is homogeneous differential equation. ∴ Put y = v x ⇒ d y d x = v + d v d x ∴ v + x d v d x = v x x + e v x x ⇒ v + x d v d x = v + e v ⇒ x d v d x = e v ⇒ e - v d v = 1 x d x On integrating both sides, we get − e − v = log x + c - e - y x = log ⁡ x + c Given, y 1 = 0 ∴ e - 0 1 = log ⁡ 1 + c - 1 = 0 + c ⇒ c = - 1 ∴ - e - y x = log ⁡ x - 1 ⇒ 1 = log ⁡ x + e - y x
questions.solution: Solution Given differential equation is x d y d x = y + x e y x d y d x = y x + e y x It is homogeneous differential equation. ∴ Put y = v x ⇒ d y d x = v + d v d x ∴ v + x d v d x = v x x + e v x x ⇒ v + x d v d x = v + e v ⇒ x d v d x = e v ⇒ e - v d v = 1 x d x On integrating both sides, we get − e − v = log x + c - e - y x = log ⁡ x + c Given, y 1 = 0 ∴ e - 0 1 = log ⁡ 1 + c - 1 = 0 + c ⇒ c = - 1 ∴ - e - y x = log ⁡ x - 1 ⇒ 1 = log ⁡ x + e - y x
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Given differential equation is xdydx=y+xeyx
dydx=yx+eyx
It is homogeneous differential equation.
∴ Put y=vx ⇒ dydx=v+dvdx
∴ v+xdvdx=vxx+evxx
⇒ v+xdvdx=v+ev
⇒ xdvdx=ev
⇒ e-vdv=1xdx
On integrating both sides, we get
−e−v=logx+c
-e-yx=log⁡x+c
Given, y1=0
∴ e-01=log⁡1+c
-1=0+c ⇒ c= -1
∴ -e-yx=log⁡x-1
⇒ 1=log⁡x+e-yx

questions.html: If fx and gx are periodic functions with period 7 and 11, respectively. Then, the period of Fx=fxgx5-gxfx3 is
questions.questionHtml: If fx and gx are periodic functions with period 7 and 11, respectively. Then, the period of Fx=fxgx5-gxfx3 is
questions.solutionText: Solution The period of f x is 7 ⇒ The period of f x 3 is 7 1 3 = 21 The period of g x is 11 ⇒ The period of g x 5 is 11 1 5 = 55 Hence, T 1 = period of f x g x 5 = 7 × 55 = 385 and T 2 = period of g x f x 3 = 11 × 21 = 231 ∴ Period of F x = LCM T 1 , T 2 = LCM 385 , 231 = 7 × 11 × 3 × 5 = 1155
questions.solution: Solution The period of f x is 7 ⇒ The period of f x 3 is 7 1 3 = 21 The period of g x is 11 ⇒ The period of g x 5 is 11 1 5 = 55 Hence, T 1 = period of f x g x 5 = 7 × 55 = 385 and T 2 = period of g x f x 3 = 11 × 21 = 231 ∴ Period of F x = LCM T 1 , T 2 = LCM 385 , 231 = 7 × 11 × 3 × 5 = 1155
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
The period of fx is 7⇒The period of fx3 is 713=21
The period of gx is 11⇒The period of gx5 is 1115=55
Hence, T1= period of fxgx5=7×55=385 and T2=period of gxfx3=11×21=231
∴Period of Fx=LCMT1,T2=LCM385,231
=7×11×3×5=1155

questions.questionHtml: The differential equation representing the family of curves y=a⋅e3x+b⋅e−2x, where a and b are arbitrary constants, is:
questions.solutionText: Solution y = a e 3 x + b e − 2 x y ′ = 3 a e 3 x − 2 b e − 2 x y ″ = 9 a e 3 x + 4 b e − 2 x y ″ − y ′ − 6 y = ( 9 a e 3 x + 4 b e − 2 x ) − ( 3 a e 3 x − 2 b e − 2 x ) − 6 ( a e 3 x + b e − 2 x ) = a e 3 x ( 9 − 3 − 6 ) + b e − 2 x ( 4 + 2 − 6 ) = 0 Answer: B
questions.solution: Solution y = a e 3 x + b e − 2 x y ′ = 3 a e 3 x − 2 b e − 2 x y ″ = 9 a e 3 x + 4 b e − 2 x y ″ − y ′ − 6 y = ( 9 a e 3 x + 4 b e − 2 x ) − ( 3 a e 3 x − 2 b e − 2 x ) − 6 ( a e 3 x + b e − 2 x ) = a e 3 x ( 9 − 3 − 6 ) + b e − 2 x ( 4 + 2 − 6 ) = 0 Answer: B
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
y=ae3x+be−2x

y′=3ae3x−2be−2x

y″=9ae3x+4be−2x

y″−y′−6y=(9ae3x+4be−2x)−(3ae3x−2be−2x)−6(ae3x+be−2x)

=ae3x(9−3−6)+be−2x(4+2−6)=0

Answer: B

questions.solutionText: Solution We have given that ∫ 0 1 e sin x d x = λ f ( x ) = e sinx f ( x ) is a periodic function, its value will repeat as sinx repeat its value. ∴ Period of f ( x ) = 2 π ∫ 0 2 0 0 e sin x d x = ∫ 0 1 0 0 π · 2 π e sin x d x But 1 0 0 π is not an integer ∴ Statement I is wrong. and ∫ 0 n a f x d x = n ∫ 0 a f x d x , n ∈ I So Statement II is true.
questions.solution: Solution We have given that ∫ 0 1 e sin x d x = λ f ( x ) = e sinx f ( x ) is a periodic function, its value will repeat as sinx repeat its value. ∴ Period of f ( x ) = 2 π ∫ 0 2 0 0 e sin x d x = ∫ 0 1 0 0 π · 2 π e sin x d x But 1 0 0 π is not an integer ∴ Statement I is wrong. and ∫ 0 n a f x d x = n ∫ 0 a f x d x , n ∈ I So Statement II is true.

Solution

We have given that ∫01esinxdx=λ
f(x)=esinx
f(x) is a periodic function, its value will repeat as sinx repeat its value.
∴Period of f(x)=2π
∫0200esinxdx=∫0100π·2πesinxdx
But 100π is not an integer
∴ Statement I is wrong.

and ∫0nafxdx=n∫0afxdx, n∈I
So Statement II is true.

questions.questionText: ∫ cos x - sin x 7 - 9 sin 2 x d x , is equal to

questions.prompt: ∫ cos x - sin x 7 - 9 sin 2 x d x , is equal to
questions.html: ∫cosx-sinx7-9sin2xdx, is equal to
questions.questionHtml: ∫cosx-sinx7-9sin2xdx, is equal to
questions.solutionText: Solution ∫ cos x - sin x 7 - 9 sin 2 x d x = ∫ cos x - sin x 7 - 9 [ sin x + cos x 2 - 1 ] d x Put, sin x + cos x = t ⇒ cos x - sin x d x = d t ⇒ I = ∫ d t 7 - 9 t 2 - 1 = ∫ d t 4 2 - 3 t 2 = 1 2 · 4 · 1 3 ln 4 + 3 t 4 - 3 t + c = 1 2 4 log 4 + 3 sin x + cos x 4 - 3 sin x + cos x + c , where c is the constant of integration.
questions.solution: Solution ∫ cos x - sin x 7 - 9 sin 2 x d x = ∫ cos x - sin x 7 - 9 [ sin x + cos x 2 - 1 ] d x Put, sin x + cos x = t ⇒ cos x - sin x d x = d t ⇒ I = ∫ d t 7 - 9 t 2 - 1 = ∫ d t 4 2 - 3 t 2 = 1 2 · 4 · 1 3 ln 4 + 3 t 4 - 3 t + c = 1 2 4 log 4 + 3 sin x + cos x 4 - 3 sin x + cos x + c , where c is the constant of integration.
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
∫cosx-sinx7-9sin2xdx
=∫cosx-sinx 7-9[sinx+cosx 2-1]dx
Put, sinx+cosx=t⇒cosx-sinxdx=dt
⇒I=∫dt7-9t2-1=∫dt42-3t2
=12·4·13ln4+3t4-3t+c
=124log4+3sinx+cosx4-3sinx+cosx+c, where c is the constant of integration.

questions.questionText: If x = 2 sin θ - sin 2 θ and y = 2 cos θ - cos 2 θ , θ ∈ 0 , 2 π , then d 2 y d x 2 at θ = π is:

questions.prompt: If x = 2 sin θ - sin 2 θ and y = 2 cos θ - cos 2 θ , θ ∈ 0 , 2 π , then d 2 y d x 2 at θ = π is:
questions.html: If x=2sinθ-sin2θ and y=2cosθ-cos2θ, θ∈0,2π, then d2ydx2 at θ=π is:
questions.questionHtml: If x=2sinθ-sin2θ and y=2cosθ-cos2θ, θ∈0,2π, then d2ydx2 at θ=π is:
questions.solutionText: Solution d x d θ = 2 cos ⁡ θ − 2 cos ⁡ 2 θ , d y d θ = − 2 sin ⁡ θ + 2 sin ⁡ 2 θ d y d x = sin ⁡ 2 θ − sin ⁡ θ cos ⁡ θ − cos ⁡ 2 θ At θ = π : d x d θ = 2 ( − 1 ) − 2 ( 1 ) = − 4 Let N = sin ⁡ 2 θ − sin ⁡ θ , D = cos ⁡ θ − cos ⁡ 2 θ N | π = 0 − 0 = 0 , D | π = − 1 − 1 = − 2 N ′ = 2 cos ⁡ 2 θ − cos ⁡ θ , D ′ = − sin ⁡ θ + 2 sin ⁡ 2 θ N ′ | π = 2 ( 1 ) − ( − 1 ) = 3 , D ′ | π = 0 d d θ ( d y d x ) = N ′ D − N D ′ D 2 = 3 × ( − 2 ) − 0 4 = − 3 2 d 2 y d x 2 = − 3 / 2 − 4 = 3 8 Answer: (2) 3 8
questions.solution: Solution d x d θ = 2 cos ⁡ θ − 2 cos ⁡ 2 θ , d y d θ = − 2 sin ⁡ θ + 2 sin ⁡ 2 θ d y d x = sin ⁡ 2 θ − sin ⁡ θ cos ⁡ θ − cos ⁡ 2 θ At θ = π : d x d θ = 2 ( − 1 ) − 2 ( 1 ) = − 4 Let N = sin ⁡ 2 θ − sin ⁡ θ , D = cos ⁡ θ − cos ⁡ 2 θ N | π = 0 − 0 = 0 , D | π = − 1 − 1 = − 2 N ′ = 2 cos ⁡ 2 θ − cos ⁡ θ , D ′ = − sin ⁡ θ + 2 sin ⁡ 2 θ N ′ | π = 2 ( 1 ) − ( − 1 ) = 3 , D ′ | π = 0 d d θ ( d y d x ) = N ′ D − N D ′ D 2 = 3 × ( − 2 ) − 0 4 = − 3 2 d 2 y d x 2 = − 3 / 2 − 4 = 3 8 Answer: (2) 3 8
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
dxdθ=2cos⁡θ−2cos⁡2θ,dydθ=−2sin⁡θ+2sin⁡2θ

dydx=sin⁡2θ−sin⁡θcos⁡θ−cos⁡2θ

At θ=π:
dxdθ=2(−1)−2(1)=−4

Let N=sin⁡2θ−sin⁡θ, D=cos⁡θ−cos⁡2θ

N|π=0−0=0,D|π=−1−1=−2

N′=2cos⁡2θ−cos⁡θ,D′=−sin⁡θ+2sin⁡2θ

N′|π=2(1)−(−1)=3,D′|π=0

ddθ(dydx)=N′D−ND′D2=3×(−2)−04=−32

d2ydx2=−3/2−4=38

Answer: (2) 38

questions.questionHtml: The value of nC0−nC1+nC2…….+(−1)n nCn is
questions.solutionText: Solution Using the binomial expansion of ( 1 − 1 ) n : ( 1 + x ) n = ∑ r = 0 n ( n r ) x r Put x = − 1 : ( 1 − 1 ) n = ( n 0 ) − ( n 1 ) + ( n 2 ) − ⋯ + ( − 1 ) n ( n n ) 0 = n C 0 − n C 1 + n C 2 − ⋯ + ( − 1 ) n n C n Answer: (4) 0
questions.solution: Solution Using the binomial expansion of ( 1 − 1 ) n : ( 1 + x ) n = ∑ r = 0 n ( n r ) x r Put x = − 1 : ( 1 − 1 ) n = ( n 0 ) − ( n 1 ) + ( n 2 ) − ⋯ + ( − 1 ) n ( n n ) 0 = n C 0 − n C 1 + n C 2 − ⋯ + ( − 1 ) n n C n Answer: (4) 0
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Using the binomial expansion of (1−1)n:

(1+x)n=∑r=0n(nr)xr

Put x=−1:
(1−1)n=(n0)−(n1)+(n2)−⋯+(−1)n(nn)

0=nC0−nC1+nC2−⋯+(−1)n nCn

Answer: (4) 0

questions.html: There are 10 red and 5 yellow roses of different sizes. If x is the number of garlands that can be formed with all these flowers so that no two yellow roses come together and y is the number of garlands formed with all these flowers so that all the red roses coming together, then 2x-y10!=
questions.questionHtml: There are 10 red and 5 yellow roses of different sizes. If x is the number of garlands that can be formed with all these flowers so that no two yellow roses come together and y is the number of garlands formed with all these flowers so that all the red roses coming together, then 2x-y10!=
questions.solutionText: Solution Given that 10 Red roses & 5 Yellow roses. x is number of garlands that can be formed with all these flowers so that no two yellow roses come together. i.e., first arranging 10 Red roses in 9 ! 2 then in that 10 gaps we can arrange these 5 yellow roses in 10 ! 5 ! = 10 × 9 × 8 × 7 × 6 . So x = 9 ! 2 × 10 P 5 y is number of garlands with all these flowers so that all the red roses coming together. i.e., taking all red roses as one unit and arranging red and yellow roses is y = 5 ! 2 × 10 ! . 2 x - y 10 ! = 10 P 5 10 - 5 ! ∴ 2 x - y 10 ! = 9 ! 5 ! - 5 !
questions.solution: Solution Given that 10 Red roses & 5 Yellow roses. x is number of garlands that can be formed with all these flowers so that no two yellow roses come together. i.e., first arranging 10 Red roses in 9 ! 2 then in that 10 gaps we can arrange these 5 yellow roses in 10 ! 5 ! = 10 × 9 × 8 × 7 × 6 . So x = 9 ! 2 × 10 P 5 y is number of garlands with all these flowers so that all the red roses coming together. i.e., taking all red roses as one unit and arranging red and yellow roses is y = 5 ! 2 × 10 ! . 2 x - y 10 ! = 10 P 5 10 - 5 ! ∴ 2 x - y 10 ! = 9 ! 5 ! - 5 !
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution

Given that

10 Red roses & 5 Yellow roses.

x is number of garlands that can be formed with all these flowers so that no two yellow roses come together.

i.e., first arranging 10 Red roses in 9!2 then in that 10 gaps we can arrange these 5 yellow roses in 10!5!=10×9×8×7×6.

So x=9!2×10P5

y is number of garlands with all these flowers so that all the red roses coming together.

i.e., taking all red roses as one unit and arranging red and yellow roses is y=5!2×10!.

2x-y10!=10P510-5!

∴2x-y10!=9!5!-5!

questions.question: A real valued differentiable function f satisfies f ( x ) + f ( y ) + 2 x y = f ( x + y ) ∀ x , y ∈ R . Given f ′ ( 0 ) = 0 , then value of ∫ 0 π 2 f ( sin ⁡ x ) d x will be
questions.prompt: A real valued differentiable function f satisfies f ( x ) + f ( y ) + 2 x y = f ( x + y ) ∀ x , y ∈ R . Given f ′ ( 0 ) = 0 , then value of ∫ 0 π 2 f ( sin ⁡ x ) d x will be
questions.html: A real valued differentiable function f satisfies f(x)+f(y)+2xy=f(x+y)∀x,y∈R. Given f′(0)=0, then value of ∫0π2f(sin⁡x)dx will be
questions.questionHtml: A real valued differentiable function f satisfies f(x)+f(y)+2xy=f(x+y)∀x,y∈R. Given f′(0)=0, then value of ∫0π2f(sin⁡x)dx will be
questions.solutionText: Solution f ( x ) + f ( y ) + 2 x y = f ( x + y ) . . . ( i ) differentiating the equation, ⇒ f ′ ( y ) + 2 x = f ′ ( x + y ) at y = 0 f ′ ( 0 ) + 2 x = f ′ ( x ) ⇒ f ′ ( x ) = 2 x Integrating both sides. ∫ f ′ ( x ) = ∫ 2 x ⋅ d x ⇒ f ( x ) = x 2 ∴ I = ∫ 0 π 2 f ( sin ⁡ x ) d x ⇒ I = ∫ 0 π 2 sin 2 ⁡ x ⋅ d x . . . ( i i ) ⇒ I = ∫ 0 π 2 sin 2 ⁡ ( π 2 − x ) d x = ∫ 0 π 2 cos 2 ⁡ x ⋅ d x … ( i i i ) Adding Eqs. (ii) and (iii) 2 l = ∫ 0 π 2 ( sin 2 ⁡ x + cos 2 ⁡ x ) d x = ∫ 0 π 2 1 ⋅ d x ⇒ 2 l = π 2 ⇒ l = π 4
questions.solution: Solution f ( x ) + f ( y ) + 2 x y = f ( x + y ) . . . ( i ) differentiating the equation, ⇒ f ′ ( y ) + 2 x = f ′ ( x + y ) at y = 0 f ′ ( 0 ) + 2 x = f ′ ( x ) ⇒ f ′ ( x ) = 2 x Integrating both sides. ∫ f ′ ( x ) = ∫ 2 x ⋅ d x ⇒ f ( x ) = x 2 ∴ I = ∫ 0 π 2 f ( sin ⁡ x ) d x ⇒ I = ∫ 0 π 2 sin 2 ⁡ x ⋅ d x . . . ( i i ) ⇒ I = ∫ 0 π 2 sin 2 ⁡ ( π 2 − x ) d x = ∫ 0 π 2 cos 2 ⁡ x ⋅ d x … ( i i i ) Adding Eqs. (ii) and (iii) 2 l = ∫ 0 π 2 ( sin 2 ⁡ x + cos 2 ⁡ x ) d x = ∫ 0 π 2 1 ⋅ d x ⇒ 2 l = π 2 ⇒ l = π 4
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
f(x)+f(y)+2xy=f(x+y)...(i)
differentiating the equation,
⇒f′(y)+2x=f′(x+y)
at y=0
f′(0)+2x=f′(x)
⇒f′(x)=2x
Integrating both sides.
∫f′(x)=∫2x⋅dx
⇒f(x)=x2
∴I=∫0π2f(sin⁡x)dx
⇒I=∫0π2sin2⁡x⋅dx...(ii)
⇒I=∫0π2sin2⁡(π2−x)dx=∫0π2cos2⁡x⋅dx…(iii)
Adding Eqs. (ii) and (iii)
2l=∫0π2(sin2⁡x+cos2⁡x)dx=∫0π21⋅dx
⇒2l=π2⇒l=π4

questions.text: The integral ∫ d x ( x + 4 ) 8 7 ( x - 3 ) 6 7 is equal to: (where C is a constant of integration)
questions.questionText: The integral ∫ d x ( x + 4 ) 8 7 ( x - 3 ) 6 7 is equal to: (where C is a constant of integration)
questions.question: The integral ∫ d x ( x + 4 ) 8 7 ( x - 3 ) 6 7 is equal to: (where C is a constant of integration)
questions.prompt: The integral ∫ d x ( x + 4 ) 8 7 ( x - 3 ) 6 7 is equal to: (where C is a constant of integration)
questions.html: The integral ∫dx(x+4)87(x-3)67 is equal to: (where C is a constant of integration)
questions.questionHtml: The integral ∫dx(x+4)87(x-3)67 is equal to: (where C is a constant of integration)
questions.solutionText: Solution ∫ d x ( x + 4 ) 8 7 ( x − 3 ) 6 7 Divide numerator and denominator by ( x + 4 ) 2 : = ∫ d x ( x + 4 ) 2 ⋅ ( x − 3 x + 4 ) 6 7 Let t = x − 3 x + 4 d t d x = ( x + 4 ) − ( x − 3 ) ( x + 4 ) 2 = 7 ( x + 4 ) 2 ⇒ d x ( x + 4 ) 2 = d t 7 ∫ d t 7 ⋅ t 6 / 7 = 1 7 ∫ t − 6 / 7 d t = 1 7 ⋅ t 1 / 7 1 / 7 + C = t 1 / 7 + C = ( x − 3 x + 4 ) 1 7 + C Answer: (1) ( x − 3 x + 4 ) 1 7 + C
questions.solution: Solution ∫ d x ( x + 4 ) 8 7 ( x − 3 ) 6 7 Divide numerator and denominator by ( x + 4 ) 2 : = ∫ d x ( x + 4 ) 2 ⋅ ( x − 3 x + 4 ) 6 7 Let t = x − 3 x + 4 d t d x = ( x + 4 ) − ( x − 3 ) ( x + 4 ) 2 = 7 ( x + 4 ) 2 ⇒ d x ( x + 4 ) 2 = d t 7 ∫ d t 7 ⋅ t 6 / 7 = 1 7 ∫ t − 6 / 7 d t = 1 7 ⋅ t 1 / 7 1 / 7 + C = t 1 / 7 + C = ( x − 3 x + 4 ) 1 7 + C Answer: (1) ( x − 3 x + 4 ) 1 7 + C
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
∫dx(x+4)87(x−3)67

Divide numerator and denominator by (x+4)2:

=∫dx(x+4)2⋅(x−3x+4)67

Let t=x−3x+4

dtdx=(x+4)−(x−3)(x+4)2=7(x+4)2

⇒dx(x+4)2=dt7

∫dt7⋅t6/7=17∫t−6/7 dt=17⋅t1/71/7+C=t1/7+C

=(x−3x+4)17+C

Answer: (1) (x−3x+4)17+C

questions.text: Let A be a finite set such that the total number of reflexive relations that can be defined on A is 4096. What is the number of elements in the power set of A ?
questions.questionText: Let A be a finite set such that the total number of reflexive relations that can be defined on A is 4096. What is the number of elements in the power set of A ?
questions.question: Let A be a finite set such that the total number of reflexive relations that can be defined on A is 4096. What is the number of elements in the power set of A ?
questions.prompt: Let A be a finite set such that the total number of reflexive relations that can be defined on A is 4096. What is the number of elements in the power set of A ?
questions.html: Let A be a finite set such that the total number of reflexive relations that can be defined on A is 4096. What is the number of elements in the power set of A?
questions.questionHtml: Let A be a finite set such that the total number of reflexive relations that can be defined on A is 4096. What is the number of elements in the power set of A?
questions.solutionText: Solution Number of reflexive relations on a set with n elements = 2 n 2 − n Given: 2 n 2 − n = 4096 = 2 12 n 2 − n = 12 n 2 − n − 12 = 0 ( n − 4 ) ( n + 3 ) = 0 n = 4 Number of elements in power set of A = 2 n = 2 4 = 16 Answer: D. 16
questions.solution: Solution Number of reflexive relations on a set with n elements = 2 n 2 − n Given: 2 n 2 − n = 4096 = 2 12 n 2 − n = 12 n 2 − n − 12 = 0 ( n − 4 ) ( n + 3 ) = 0 n = 4 Number of elements in power set of A = 2 n = 2 4 = 16 Answer: D. 16
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Number of reflexive relations on a set with n elements = 2n2−n

Given: 2n2−n=4096=212

n2−n=12
n2−n−12=0
(n−4)(n+3)=0
n=4

Number of elements in power set of A = 2n=24=16

Answer: D. 16

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: Determinant of second order is made with the element 0 and 1. The number of determinants with non negative values is
questions.questionText: Determinant of second order is made with the element 0 and 1. The number of determinants with non negative values is
questions.question: Determinant of second order is made with the element 0 and 1. The number of determinants with non negative values is
questions.prompt: Determinant of second order is made with the element 0 and 1. The number of determinants with non negative values is
questions.html: Determinant of second order is made with the element 0 and 1. The number of determinants with non negative values is
questions.questionHtml: Determinant of second order is made with the element 0 and 1. The number of determinants with non negative values is
questions.solutionText: Solution A second order determinant | a b c d | = a d − b c where a , b , c , d ∈ { 0 , 1 } Total determinants possible = 2 4 = 16 List all cases where a d − b c < 0 , i.e., a d − b c = − 1 (only possible negative value): a d = 0 and b c = 1 ⇒ b = c = 1 and at least one of a , d = 0 When b = 1 , c = 1 : ( a , d ) can be ( 0 , 0 ) , ( 0 , 1 ) , ( 1 , 0 ) ⇒ a d − b c = − 1 for all three. So 3 determinants have negative values. Non-negative determinants = 16 − 3 = 13 Answer: (4) 13
questions.solution: Solution A second order determinant | a b c d | = a d − b c where a , b , c , d ∈ { 0 , 1 } Total determinants possible = 2 4 = 16 List all cases where a d − b c < 0 , i.e., a d − b c = − 1 (only possible negative value): a d = 0 and b c = 1 ⇒ b = c = 1 and at least one of a , d = 0 When b = 1 , c = 1 : ( a , d ) can be ( 0 , 0 ) , ( 0 , 1 ) , ( 1 , 0 ) ⇒ a d − b c = − 1 for all three. So 3 determinants have negative values. Non-negative determinants = 16 − 3 = 13 Answer: (4) 13
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
A second order determinant |abcd|=ad−bc where a,b,c,d∈{0,1}

Total determinants possible =24=16

List all cases where ad−bc<0, i.e., ad−bc=−1 (only possible negative value):
ad=0 and bc=1 ⇒ b=c=1 and at least one of a,d=0

When b=1,c=1: (a,d) can be (0,0),(0,1),(1,0) ⇒ ad−bc=−1 for all three.

So 3 determinants have negative values.

Non-negative determinants =16−3=13

Answer: (4) 13

questions.prompt: If the slope of one of the line represented by the equation a x 2 + 2 h x y + b y 2 = 0 be λ times that of the other, then
questions.html: If the slope of one of the line represented by the equation ax2+2hxy+by2=0 be λ times that of the other, then
questions.questionHtml: If the slope of one of the line represented by the equation ax2+2hxy+by2=0 be λ times that of the other, then
questions.solutionText: Solution It is given that m 2 = λ m 1 ⇒ m 1 + λ m 1 = − 2 h b ⇒ m 1 = − 2 h b ( 1 + λ ) ...(i) and m 1 ⋅ λ m 1 = a b ⇒ m 1 = a b λ ...(ii) Hence, by (i) and (ii), a b λ = − 2 h b ( 1 + λ ) On squaring both sides, we get 4 λ h 2 = a b ( 1 + λ ) 2 .
questions.solution: Solution It is given that m 2 = λ m 1 ⇒ m 1 + λ m 1 = − 2 h b ⇒ m 1 = − 2 h b ( 1 + λ ) ...(i) and m 1 ⋅ λ m 1 = a b ⇒ m 1 = a b λ ...(ii) Hence, by (i) and (ii), a b λ = − 2 h b ( 1 + λ ) On squaring both sides, we get 4 λ h 2 = a b ( 1 + λ ) 2 .
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
It is given that m2=λm1⇒m1+λm1=−2hb
⇒m1=−2hb(1+λ)...(i)
and m1⋅λm1=ab⇒m1=abλ...(ii)
Hence, by (i) and (ii), abλ=−2hb(1+λ)
On squaring both sides, we get 4λh2=ab(1+λ)2.

questions.text: The Geometric Mean of the numbers 3 , 3 2 , 3 3 … 3 n is
questions.questionText: The Geometric Mean of the numbers 3 , 3 2 , 3 3 … 3 n is
questions.question: The Geometric Mean of the numbers 3 , 3 2 , 3 3 … 3 n is
questions.prompt: The Geometric Mean of the numbers 3 , 3 2 , 3 3 … 3 n is
questions.html: The Geometric Mean of the numbers 3,32,33…3n is
questions.questionHtml: The Geometric Mean of the numbers 3,32,33…3n is
questions.solutionText: Solution GM of n numbers = ( ∏ k = 1 n 3 k ) 1 / n ∏ k = 1 n 3 k = 3 1 + 2 + 3 + ⋯ + n = 3 n ( n + 1 ) 2 G M = ( 3 n ( n + 1 ) 2 ) 1 / n = 3 n + 1 2 = 3 ( n + 1 ) / 2 Answer: (4) 3 ( n + 1 ) / 2
questions.solution: Solution GM of n numbers = ( ∏ k = 1 n 3 k ) 1 / n ∏ k = 1 n 3 k = 3 1 + 2 + 3 + ⋯ + n = 3 n ( n + 1 ) 2 G M = ( 3 n ( n + 1 ) 2 ) 1 / n = 3 n + 1 2 = 3 ( n + 1 ) / 2 Answer: (4) 3 ( n + 1 ) / 2
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
GM of n numbers =(∏k=1n3k)1/n

∏k=1n3k=31+2+3+⋯+n=3n(n+1)2

GM=(3n(n+1)2)1/n=3n+12=3(n+1)/2

Answer: (4) 3(n+1)/2

questions.prompt: The number of solution(s) of the equation z 2 = 4 z + z 2 + 16 z 3 ( z is a complex number) is/are: (where z = x + i y , x , y ∈ R , i 2 = – 1 a n d x ≠ 2 )
questions.html: The number of solution(s) of the equation z2=4z + z2 +16z3 (z is a complex number) is/are: (where z = x+iy, x,y∈R, i2 = –1 and x≠ 2)
questions.questionHtml: The number of solution(s) of the equation z2=4z + z2 +16z3 (z is a complex number) is/are: (where z = x+iy, x,y∈R, i2 = –1 and x≠ 2)
questions.solutionText: Solution The given equation is z 2 = 4 z + z 2 + 16 z 3 z = x + i y , x , y ∈ R , i 2 = – 1 and x ≠ 2 The given equation can be written as z 2 - 4 z = z 2 + 16 z 3 . . . . 1 Now, taking conjugate on both sides, we get z 2 - 4 z ¯ = z 2 + 16 z 3 ¯ ⇒ z ¯ 2 - 4 z ¯ = z ¯ 2 + 16 z ¯ 3 ⇒ z ¯ 2 - 4 z ¯ = z 2 + 16 z 3 . . . . 2 Since, z = z ¯ From equations 1 and 2 , we have z 2 - 4 z = z ¯ 2 - 4 z ¯ ⇒ z 2 - z ¯ 2 = 4 z - 4 z ¯ ⇒ z - z ¯ z + z ¯ = 4 z - z ¯ ⇒ z - z ¯ z + z ¯ - 4 z - z ¯ = 0 ⇒ z - z ¯ z + z ¯ - 4 = 0 Either z - z ¯ = 0 or z + z ¯ = 4 z = z ¯ ⇒ Im z = 0 which is not a complex number. z + z ¯ = 4 ⇒ x = 2 which is not possible. Hence, there is no solution.
questions.solution: Solution The given equation is z 2 = 4 z + z 2 + 16 z 3 z = x + i y , x , y ∈ R , i 2 = – 1 and x ≠ 2 The given equation can be written as z 2 - 4 z = z 2 + 16 z 3 . . . . 1 Now, taking conjugate on both sides, we get z 2 - 4 z ¯ = z 2 + 16 z 3 ¯ ⇒ z ¯ 2 - 4 z ¯ = z ¯ 2 + 16 z ¯ 3 ⇒ z ¯ 2 - 4 z ¯ = z 2 + 16 z 3 . . . . 2 Since, z = z ¯ From equations 1 and 2 , we have z 2 - 4 z = z ¯ 2 - 4 z ¯ ⇒ z 2 - z ¯ 2 = 4 z - 4 z ¯ ⇒ z - z ¯ z + z ¯ = 4 z - z ¯ ⇒ z - z ¯ z + z ¯ - 4 z - z ¯ = 0 ⇒ z - z ¯ z + z ¯ - 4 = 0 Either z - z ¯ = 0 or z + z ¯ = 4 z = z ¯ ⇒ Im z = 0 which is not a complex number. z + z ¯ = 4 ⇒ x = 2 which is not possible. Hence, there is no solution.
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution

The given equation is z2=4z + z2 +16z3

z = x+iy, x,y∈R, i2 = –1 and x≠ 2

The given equation can be written as z2-4z = z2 +16z3....1

Now, taking conjugate on both sides, we get

z2-4z¯ =z2 +16z3¯

⇒z¯2-4z¯=z¯2+16z¯3

⇒z¯2-4z¯=z2+16z3....2 Since, z=z¯

From equations 1 and 2, we have

z2-4z=z¯2-4z¯

⇒z2-z¯2=4z-4z¯

⇒z-z¯z+z¯=4z-z¯

⇒z-z¯z+z¯-4z-z¯=0

⇒z-z¯z+z¯-4=0

Either z-z¯=0 or z+z¯=4

z=z¯⇒Imz=0 which is not a complex number.

z+z¯=4⇒x=2 which is not possible.

Hence, there is no solution.

Hence q = - p - 2 p . . . i Since O P ⊥ O Q , ∴ 2 a p - 0 a p 2 - 0 × 2 a q - 0 a q 2 - 0 = - 1 ⇒ p q = - 4 ⇒ p - p - 2 p = - 4 [Using ( i ) ] ⇒ p 2 = 2

Solution

Since the normal at Pap2,2ap to y2=4ax meets the parabola at Qaq2, 2aq, therefore PQ is the focal chord. Hence

q=-p-2p ...i

Since OP⊥OQ,

∴ 2ap-0ap2-0×2aq-0aq2-0=-1

⇒ pq=-4

questions.questionHtml:
Let A={1,2,3,4,5} and B={a,b,c}. Let R be the set of all relations from A to B.

1. Find the total number of relations possible from A to B.
2. If a relation is chosen at random, what is the probability that it is an empty relation?
questions.solutionText: Solution | A | = 5 , | B | = 3 Total number of ordered pairs in A × B = 5 × 3 = 15 Each subset of A × B is a relation, so total relations = 2 15 Empty relation is just 1 (the empty set) Probability of empty relation = 1 2 15 Answer: A) 2 15 and 1 / 2 15
questions.solution: Solution | A | = 5 , | B | = 3 Total number of ordered pairs in A × B = 5 × 3 = 15 Each subset of A × B is a relation, so total relations = 2 15 Empty relation is just 1 (the empty set) Probability of empty relation = 1 2 15 Answer: A) 2 15 and 1 / 2 15
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
|A|=5, |B|=3

Total number of ordered pairs in A×B = 5×3=15

Each subset of A×B is a relation, so total relations = 215

Empty relation is just 1 (the empty set)

Probability of empty relation = 1215

Answer: A) 215 and 1/215

questions.solutionText: Solution Using the property ∫ a b f ( x ) d x = ∫ a b f ( a + b − x ) d x For I 2 , replace x → π 4 + π 2 − x = 3 π 4 − x ... Actually, add I 1 + I 2 : I 1 + I 2 = ∫ π / 4 π / 2 e sin ⁡ x + 1 e cos ⁡ x + 1 d x + ∫ π / 4 π / 2 e cos ⁡ x + 1 e sin ⁡ x + 1 d x = ∫ π / 4 π / 2 ( e sin ⁡ x + 1 ) ( e sin ⁡ x + 1 ) + ( e cos ⁡ x + 1 ) ( e cos ⁡ x + 1 ) ( e cos ⁡ x + 1 ) ( e sin ⁡ x + 1 ) d x ... Simpler: I 1 + I 2 = ∫ π / 4 π / 2 [ e sin ⁡ x + 1 e cos ⁡ x + 1 + e cos ⁡ x + 1 e sin ⁡ x + 1 ] d x Each integrand term is positive and > 0 , so I 1 + I 2 > 0 , ruling out option (4). On [ π 4 , π 2 ] : sin ⁡ x ≥ cos ⁡ x ⇒ e sin ⁡ x ≥ e cos ⁡ x ⇒ e sin ⁡ x + 1 e cos ⁡ x + 1 ≥ e cos ⁡ x + 1 e sin ⁡ x + 1 ⇒ I 1 ≥ I 2 , with equality only at x = π 4 , so I 1 > I 2 Answer: (1) I 1 > I 2
questions.solution: Solution Using the property ∫ a b f ( x ) d x = ∫ a b f ( a + b − x ) d x For I 2 , replace x → π 4 + π 2 − x = 3 π 4 − x ... Actually, add I 1 + I 2 : I 1 + I 2 = ∫ π / 4 π / 2 e sin ⁡ x + 1 e cos ⁡ x + 1 d x + ∫ π / 4 π / 2 e cos ⁡ x + 1 e sin ⁡ x + 1 d x = ∫ π / 4 π / 2 ( e sin ⁡ x + 1 ) ( e sin ⁡ x + 1 ) + ( e cos ⁡ x + 1 ) ( e cos ⁡ x + 1 ) ( e cos ⁡ x + 1 ) ( e sin ⁡ x + 1 ) d x ... Simpler: I 1 + I 2 = ∫ π / 4 π / 2 [ e sin ⁡ x + 1 e cos ⁡ x + 1 + e cos ⁡ x + 1 e sin ⁡ x + 1 ] d x Each integrand term is positive and > 0 , so I 1 + I 2 > 0 , ruling out option (4). On [ π 4 , π 2 ] : sin ⁡ x ≥ cos ⁡ x ⇒ e sin ⁡ x ≥ e cos ⁡ x ⇒ e sin ⁡ x + 1 e cos ⁡ x + 1 ≥ e cos ⁡ x + 1 e sin ⁡ x + 1 ⇒ I 1 ≥ I 2 , with equality only at x = π 4 , so I 1 > I 2 Answer: (1) I 1 > I 2
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Using the property ∫abf(x) dx=∫abf(a+b−x) dx

For I2, replace x→π4+π2−x=3π4−x...

Actually, add I1+I2:

I1+I2=∫π/4π/2esin⁡x+1ecos⁡x+1 dx+∫π/4π/2ecos⁡x+1esin⁡x+1 dx

=∫π/4π/2(esin⁡x+1)(esin⁡x+1)+(ecos⁡x+1)(ecos⁡x+1)(ecos⁡x+1)(esin⁡x+1) dx...

Simpler: I1+I2=∫π/4π/2[esin⁡x+1ecos⁡x+1+ecos⁡x+1esin⁡x+1]dx

Each integrand term is positive and >0, so I1+I2>0, ruling out option (4).

On [π4,π2]: sin⁡x≥cos⁡x ⇒ esin⁡x≥ecos⁡x

⇒esin⁡x+1ecos⁡x+1≥ecos⁡x+1esin⁡x+1

⇒I1≥I2, with equality only at x=π4, so I1>I2

Answer: (1) I1>I2

questions.questionHtml: Let ρ be a relation defined on N, the set of natural numbers, as ρ={(x,y)∈N×N:2x+y=41}. Then
questions.solutionText: Solution We have, ρ = { ( x , y ) ∈ N × N : 2 x + y = 41 } For reflexive, x p x ⇒ 2 x + x = 41 ⇒ 3 x = 41 ⇒ x = 41 3 ∉ N So, ρ is not reflexive. For symmetric, x ρ y ⇒ 2 x + y = 41 and y ρ x ⇒ 2 y + x = 41 ⇒ x p y ≠ y ρ x So, ρ is not symmetric. For transitive, x p y ⇒ 2 x + y = 41 and y ρ z ⇒ 2 y + z = 41 ⇒ x p z ⇒ ρ is not transitive.
questions.solution: Solution We have, ρ = { ( x , y ) ∈ N × N : 2 x + y = 41 } For reflexive, x p x ⇒ 2 x + x = 41 ⇒ 3 x = 41 ⇒ x = 41 3 ∉ N So, ρ is not reflexive. For symmetric, x ρ y ⇒ 2 x + y = 41 and y ρ x ⇒ 2 y + x = 41 ⇒ x p y ≠ y ρ x So, ρ is not symmetric. For transitive, x p y ⇒ 2 x + y = 41 and y ρ z ⇒ 2 y + z = 41 ⇒ x p z ⇒ ρ is not transitive.
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
We have, ρ={(x,y)∈N×N:2x+y=41}
For reflexive,
xpx⇒2x+x=41⇒3x=41
⇒x=413∉N
So, ρ is not reflexive.
For symmetric,
xρy⇒2x+y=41
and yρx⇒2y+x=41
⇒xpy≠yρx
So, ρ is not symmetric.
For transitive,
xpy⇒2x+y=41
and yρz⇒2y+z=41
⇒xpz
⇒ρ is not transitive.

questions.questionText: Let f be a differentiable function such that f ( 1 ) = 2 and f ′ ( x ) = f ( x ) , for all x ∈ R . If h ( x ) = f ( f ( x ) ) , then h ′ ( 1 ) is equal to

questions.questionHtml: Let f be a differentiable function such that f(1)=2 and f′(x)=f(x), for all x∈R. If h(x)=f(f(x)), then h′(1) is equal to
questions.solutionText: Solution Given: f ′ ( x ) = f ( x ) for all x ∈ R ′ ⇒ f ′ ( x ) f ( x ) = 1 Integrating on both sides, wwe get log ⁡ | f ( x ) | = x + c ⇒ f ( x ) = e x + c ⇒ f ( x ) = e x ⋅ e c ⇒ f ( x ) = e x ⋅ c 1 ... (i) [ e c = c 1 ] As f ( 1 ) = 2 ∴ c 1 ⋅ e = 2 ⇒ c 1 = 2 e Equation (i) becomes f ( x ) = e x ⋅ 2 e Now, h ( x ) = f ( f ( x ) ) ∴ h ′ ( x ) = f ′ ( f ( x ) ) × f ′ ( x ) ∴ h ′ ( 1 ) = f ′ ( f ( 1 ) ) × f ′ ( 1 ) ⇒ h ′ ( 1 ) = f ′ ( 2 ) × f ′ ( 1 ) ⇒ h ′ ( 1 ) = e 2 × 2 e × 2 ⇒ h ′ ( 1 ) = 4 e
questions.solution: Solution Given: f ′ ( x ) = f ( x ) for all x ∈ R ′ ⇒ f ′ ( x ) f ( x ) = 1 Integrating on both sides, wwe get log ⁡ | f ( x ) | = x + c ⇒ f ( x ) = e x + c ⇒ f ( x ) = e x ⋅ e c ⇒ f ( x ) = e x ⋅ c 1 ... (i) [ e c = c 1 ] As f ( 1 ) = 2 ∴ c 1 ⋅ e = 2 ⇒ c 1 = 2 e Equation (i) becomes f ( x ) = e x ⋅ 2 e Now, h ( x ) = f ( f ( x ) ) ∴ h ′ ( x ) = f ′ ( f ( x ) ) × f ′ ( x ) ∴ h ′ ( 1 ) = f ′ ( f ( 1 ) ) × f ′ ( 1 ) ⇒ h ′ ( 1 ) = f ′ ( 2 ) × f ′ ( 1 ) ⇒ h ′ ( 1 ) = e 2 × 2 e × 2 ⇒ h ′ ( 1 ) = 4 e
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Given: f′(x)=f(x) for all x∈R′
⇒f′(x)f(x)=1
Integrating on both sides, wwe get log⁡|f(x)|=x+c
⇒f(x)=ex+c
⇒f(x)=ex⋅ec
⇒f(x)=ex⋅c1... (i)[ec=c1]
As f(1)=2
∴c1⋅e=2⇒c1=2e
Equation (i) becomes
f(x)=ex⋅2e
Now, h(x)=f(f(x))
∴h′(x)=f′(f(x))×f′(x)∴h′(1)=f′(f(1))×f′(1)⇒h′(1)=f′(2)×f′(1)⇒h′(1)=e2×2e×2⇒h′(1)=4e

questions.solutionText: Solution We have 2 f x + 3 f 1 x = x 2 - 1 . . . . 1 Putting 1 x in place of x , in the above equation 3 f x + 2 f 1 x = 1 x 2 - 1 . . . . . . 2 Using 2 × 1 - 3 × 2 , we get f x = 2 x 2 + 3 1 - x 2 5 x 2 Since f - x = f x , ⇒ It is an even function.

questions.text: Let f ( x ) be defined for all x > 0 and be continuous. Let f ( x ) satisfy f ( x y ) = f ( x ) − f ( y ) for all x , y and f ( e ) = 1 , then
questions.questionText: Let f ( x ) be defined for all x > 0 and be continuous. Let f ( x ) satisfy f ( x y ) = f ( x ) − f ( y ) for all x , y and f ( e ) = 1 , then
questions.question: Let f ( x ) be defined for all x > 0 and be continuous. Let f ( x ) satisfy f ( x y ) = f ( x ) − f ( y ) for all x , y and f ( e ) = 1 , then
questions.prompt: Let f ( x ) be defined for all x > 0 and be continuous. Let f ( x ) satisfy f ( x y ) = f ( x ) − f ( y ) for all x , y and f ( e ) = 1 , then
questions.html: Let f(x) be defined for all x>0 and be continuous. Let f(x) satisfy f(xy)=f(x)−f(y) for all x,y and f(e)=1, then
questions.questionHtml: Let f(x) be defined for all x>0 and be continuous. Let f(x) satisfy f(xy)=f(x)−f(y) for all x,y and f(e)=1, then
questions.solutionText: Solution Given: f ( x y ) = f ( x ) − f ( y ) and f ( e ) = 1 Put x = y : f ( 1 ) = 0 Put y = e : f ( x e ) = f ( x ) − 1 Put x = e , y = 1 t : f ( e t ) = f ( e ) + f ( t ) = 1 + f ( t ) This functional equation matches f ( x ) = ln ⁡ x since ln ⁡ ( x y ) = ln ⁡ x − ln ⁡ y and ln ⁡ e = 1 . Checking each option: (1) f ( x ) = ln ⁡ x ⇒ TRUE (2) f ( x ) = ln ⁡ x is unbounded ⇒ FALSE (3) f ( 1 x ) = ln ⁡ 1 x = − ln ⁡ x → ∞ as x → 0 + ⇒ does NOT tend to 0, FALSE (4) x f ( x ) = x ln ⁡ x → 0 as x → 0 + ⇒ tends to 0, not 1, FALSE Answer: (1) f ( x ) = ln ⁡ x
questions.solution: Solution Given: f ( x y ) = f ( x ) − f ( y ) and f ( e ) = 1 Put x = y : f ( 1 ) = 0 Put y = e : f ( x e ) = f ( x ) − 1 Put x = e , y = 1 t : f ( e t ) = f ( e ) + f ( t ) = 1 + f ( t ) This functional equation matches f ( x ) = ln ⁡ x since ln ⁡ ( x y ) = ln ⁡ x − ln ⁡ y and ln ⁡ e = 1 . Checking each option: (1) f ( x ) = ln ⁡ x ⇒ TRUE (2) f ( x ) = ln ⁡ x is unbounded ⇒ FALSE (3) f ( 1 x ) = ln ⁡ 1 x = − ln ⁡ x → ∞ as x → 0 + ⇒ does NOT tend to 0, FALSE (4) x f ( x ) = x ln ⁡ x → 0 as x → 0 + ⇒ tends to 0, not 1, FALSE Answer: (1) f ( x ) = ln ⁡ x
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Given: f(xy)=f(x)−f(y) and f(e)=1

Put x=y: f(1)=0
Put y=e: f(xe)=f(x)−1
Put x=e,y=1t: f(et)=f(e)+f(t)=1+f(t)

This functional equation matches f(x)=ln⁡x since ln⁡(xy)=ln⁡x−ln⁡y and ln⁡e=1.

Checking each option:
(1) f(x)=ln⁡x ⇒ TRUE
(2) f(x)=ln⁡x is unbounded ⇒ FALSE
(3) f(1x)=ln⁡1x=−ln⁡x→∞ as x→0+ ⇒ does NOT tend to 0, FALSE
(4) xf(x)=xln⁡x→0 as x→0+ ⇒ tends to 0, not 1, FALSE

Answer: (1) f(x)=ln⁡x

questions.text: If y = cos x + sin x , then d y d x at x = 2 π 3 is

questions.html: If y=cosx+sinx, then dydx at x=2π3 is
questions.questionHtml: If y=cosx+sinx, then dydx at x=2π3 is
questions.solutionText: Solution Given y = cos x + sin x As we know that from properties of modulus function x = x , x > 0 - x , x < 0 Also, cos x < 0 and sin x > 0 in π 2 , π So, cos x = - cos x and sin x = sin x Hence, y = - cos x + sin x , π 2 < x < π Differentiate above equation with respect to x , d y d x = d d x - cos x + sin x , π 2 < x < π d y d x = sin x + cos x , π 2 < x < π ⇒ d y d x x = 2 π 3 = sin 2 π 3 + cos 2 π 3 = 3 2 - 1 2
questions.solution: Solution Given y = cos x + sin x As we know that from properties of modulus function x = x , x > 0 - x , x < 0 Also, cos x < 0 and sin x > 0 in π 2 , π So, cos x = - cos x and sin x = sin x Hence, y = - cos x + sin x , π 2 < x < π Differentiate above equation with respect to x , d y d x = d d x - cos x + sin x , π 2 < x < π d y d x = sin x + cos x , π 2 < x < π ⇒ d y d x x = 2 π 3 = sin 2 π 3 + cos 2 π 3 = 3 2 - 1 2
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution

Given y=cosx+sinx

As we know that from properties of modulus function x=x, x>0-x, x<0

Also, cosx<0 and sinx>0 in π2, π

So, cosx=-cosx and sinx=sinx

Hence, y=-cosx+sinx, π2<x<π

Differentiate above equation with respect to x,

dydx=ddx-cosx+sinx, π2<x<π

dydx=sinx+cosx, π2<x<π

⇒ dydxx=2π3=sin2π3+cos2π3=32-12

Apply L'Hopital's rule: Numerator: d d x ∫ 0 x 2 sec 2 ⁡ t d t = sec 2 ⁡ ( x 2 ) ⋅ 2 x Denominator: d d x ( x sin ⁡ x ) = sin ⁡ x + x cos ⁡ x lim x → 0 2 x sec 2 ⁡ ( x 2 ) sin ⁡ x + x cos ⁡ x As x → 0 : sec 2 ⁡ ( x 2 ) → 1 , sin ⁡ x ≈ x , x cos ⁡ x ≈ x = lim x → 0 2 x x + x = 2 x 2 x = 1 Answer: (4) 1

Apply L'Hopital's rule: Numerator: d d x ∫ 0 x 2 sec 2 ⁡ t d t = sec 2 ⁡ ( x 2 ) ⋅ 2 x Denominator: d d x ( x sin ⁡ x ) = sin ⁡ x + x cos ⁡ x lim x → 0 2 x sec 2 ⁡ ( x 2 ) sin ⁡ x + x cos ⁡ x As x → 0 : sec 2 ⁡ ( x 2 ) → 1 , sin ⁡ x ≈ x , x cos ⁡ x ≈ x = lim x → 0 2 x x + x = 2 x 2 x = 1 Answer: (4) 1

Solution

Numerator: ddx∫0x2sec2⁡t dt=sec2⁡(x2)⋅2x

Denominator: ddx(xsin⁡x)=sin⁡x+xcos⁡x

=limx→02xx+x=2x2x=1

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: The minimum number of times a fair coin needs to be tossed so that the probability of getting at least two heads is at least 0 . 96 , is
questions.questionText: The minimum number of times a fair coin needs to be tossed so that the probability of getting at least two heads is at least 0 . 96 , is
questions.question: The minimum number of times a fair coin needs to be tossed so that the probability of getting at least two heads is at least 0 . 96 , is
questions.prompt: The minimum number of times a fair coin needs to be tossed so that the probability of getting at least two heads is at least 0 . 96 , is
questions.html: The minimum number of times a fair coin needs to be tossed so that the probability of getting at least two heads is at least 0.96, is
questions.questionHtml: The minimum number of times a fair coin needs to be tossed so that the probability of getting at least two heads is at least 0.96, is
questions.solutionText: Solution P ( at least 2 heads ) = 1 − P ( 0 heads ) − P ( 1 head ) ≥ 0.96 P ( 0 heads ) + P ( 1 head ) ≤ 0.04 For n tosses: 1 2 n + n 2 n ≤ 0.04 n + 1 2 n ≤ 0.04 Checking values: n = 5 : 6 32 = 0.1875 — NO n = 6 : 7 64 = 0.109 — NO n = 7 : 8 128 = 0.0625 — NO n = 8 : 9 256 = 0.0352 ≤ 0.04 — YES Answer: (1) 8
questions.solution: Solution P ( at least 2 heads ) = 1 − P ( 0 heads ) − P ( 1 head ) ≥ 0.96 P ( 0 heads ) + P ( 1 head ) ≤ 0.04 For n tosses: 1 2 n + n 2 n ≤ 0.04 n + 1 2 n ≤ 0.04 Checking values: n = 5 : 6 32 = 0.1875 — NO n = 6 : 7 64 = 0.109 — NO n = 7 : 8 128 = 0.0625 — NO n = 8 : 9 256 = 0.0352 ≤ 0.04 — YES Answer: (1) 8
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
P(at least 2 heads)=1−P(0 heads)−P(1 head)≥0.96

P(0 heads)+P(1 head)≤0.04

For n tosses: 12n+n2n≤0.04

n+12n≤0.04

Checking values:
n=5: 632=0.1875 — NO
n=6: 764=0.109 — NO
n=7: 8128=0.0625 — NO
n=8: 9256=0.0352≤0.04 — YES

Answer: (1) 8

questions.html: The perpendicular distance of the origin from the line 3x+4y+1=0 is

questions.solutionText: Solution Distance from point ( x 1 , y 1 ) to line a x + b y + c = 0 is | a x 1 + b y 1 + c | a 2 + b 2 Here ( x 1 , y 1 ) = ( 0 , 0 ) , line: 3 x + 4 y + 1 = 0 d = | 3 ( 0 ) + 4 ( 0 ) + 1 | 9 + 16 = 1 25 = 1 5 Answer: (4) 1 5
questions.solution: Solution Distance from point ( x 1 , y 1 ) to line a x + b y + c = 0 is | a x 1 + b y 1 + c | a 2 + b 2 Here ( x 1 , y 1 ) = ( 0 , 0 ) , line: 3 x + 4 y + 1 = 0 d = | 3 ( 0 ) + 4 ( 0 ) + 1 | 9 + 16 = 1 25 = 1 5 Answer: (4) 1 5

Distance from point (x1,y1) to line ax+by+c=0 is |ax1+by1+c|a2+b2

Answer: (4) 15

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.text: The roots of x - 41 49 + x - 49 41 + x - 2009 2009 = 0 are:
questions.questionText: The roots of x - 41 49 + x - 49 41 + x - 2009 2009 = 0 are:
questions.question: The roots of x - 41 49 + x - 49 41 + x - 2009 2009 = 0 are:
questions.prompt: The roots of x - 41 49 + x - 49 41 + x - 2009 2009 = 0 are:
questions.html: The roots of x-4149+x-4941+x-20092009=0 are:
questions.questionHtml: The roots of x-4149+x-4941+x-20092009=0 are:
questions.solutionText: Solution Given equation is: x - 41 49 + x - 49 41 + x - 2009 2009 = 0 Let, P x = x - 41 49 + x - 49 41 + x - 2009 2009 P ' x = 49 x – 41 48 + 41 x – 49 40 + 2009 x – 2009 2008 Since, all powers are even. Hence, P ' x > 0 ∀ x ∈ R ∴ P x is strictly increasing. ∴ P x cuts x -axis only once. Therefore, only one real root. Here, p 0 = - 41 49 + . . . + - 2009 2009 So, p 0 is negative. p 2009 = 2009 - 41 49 + 2009 - 49 41 + . . So, p 2009 is positive. As p 0 is negative and p x is increasing function, p x has only one positive real root.

Hence, P ' x > 0 ∀ x ∈ R ∴ P x is strictly increasing. ∴ P x cuts x -axis only once. Therefore, only one real root. Here, p 0 = - 41 49 + . . . + - 2009 2009 So, p 0 is negative. p 2009 = 2009 - 41 49 + 2009 - 49 41 + . . So, p 2009 is positive. As p 0 is negative and p x is increasing function, p x has only one positive real root.
questions.solutionHtml:
Solution
Given equation is:
x-4149+x-4941+x-20092009=0
Let, Px=x-4149+x-4941+x-20092009
P'x=49x–4148+41x–4940+2009x–20092008
Since, all powers are even.
Hence, P'x>0 ∀x∈R
∴ Px is strictly increasing.
∴ Px cuts x-axis only once.
Therefore, only one real root.
Here, p0=-4149+...+-20092009
So, p0 is negative.
p2009=2009-4149+2009-4941+..
So, p2009 is positive.
As p0 is negative and px is increasing function, px has only one positive real root.
